ARBITRATOR SCOTT ALAN GURSKY APPOINTED. ARBITRATOR'S JURISDICTION MAR-21-2016 THROUGH JUN-19-2016.

LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302: PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS ARGUED AND DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED. THE COURT FINDS THAT INDIANA LAW DOES APPLY TO THE ISSUE OF THE VIABILITY OF A SUBROGATION CLAIM FOR MEDICAL PAYMENTS, AND STATE FARM HAS NOT SUFFICIENTLY COMPLIED WITH INDIANA LAW TO ALLOW IT TO FILE ITS PROPOSED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR 5976

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-31-2016 Vacated

Notice Of Motion For Order Granting Leave To File A First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-22-2016 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File A First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Appearances: Ralph R. Rhoades of Tyson & Mendes (415.464.4935) for defendants and opposing parties; Shelby Poteet of Clerkin Sinclair & Mahfouz LLP (619.867.0196) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and moving party.

Argued and the Court vacates its tentative ruling and rules as follows: Denied without prejudice to being refiled. The Court finds that Indiana law does apply to the issue of the viability of a subrogation claim for medical payments, and State Farm has not sufficiently complied with Indiana law to allow it to file its proposed First Amended Complaint. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR 5976
